member.name: P:UIFramework.Adapters.PrefEntryAdapter.DescriptionText
member.summary: Sets the description text

member.name: P:UIFramework.Adapters.PrefEntryAdapter.DisplayName
member.summary: Sets the identifier text

member.name: P:UIFramework.Adapters.PrefEntryAdapter.EntryModel
member.summary: Runs when the model property has been set.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.PrefEntryAdapter.SaveAction
member.summary: This is called when the Save button is pressed. Override to create custom behaviour.
member.remarks: Generally MelonPreferences are saved from the category, not the indivial entries.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.PrefEntryAdapter.ToTomlString(System.Object)
member.summary: Serializes an object into the toml string representation of that object

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.PrefEntryAdapter.FromTomlString(System.String,System.Type)
member.summary: Parses a toml string into an object of type with its value.

member.name: T:UIFramework.Adapters.DataEntryAdapter
member.summary: Inherit this class to create your own custom entry controllers for your own input controls. TODO: Refactor this to suggest non-melon related settings storage

member.name: T:UIFramework.Adapters.TextEntryAdapter
member.summary: Base controller for text fields

member.name: P:UIFramework.Adapters.TextEntryAdapter.textField
member.summary: Returns the textfield

member.name: P:UIFramework.Adapters.TextEntryAdapter.PlaceHolderText
member.summary: Sets the placeholder text in the textField

member.name: T:UIFramework.Adapters.ListAreaAdapterBase
member.summary: Areas where UI elements are shown to the user. 1. ModButtonView list ModListAdapter 2. CategoryTabView tab top bar 3. Entries Content area

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.ListAreaAdapterBase.SetModel(UIFramework.Models.IHoldSubmodels)
member.summary: Sets the underlying data model for the current instance.
member.remarks: Calling this method updates the internal state to reflect the provided model. Subsequent operations may depend on the newly set model.
member.param: The model to associate with this instance. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.ListAreaAdapterBase.BuildFromModelList
member.summary: Clears the contents and recreates them from the submodels list in Model

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.ListAreaAdapterBase.SaveAction
member.summary: Is called when Save ButtonGo is clicked. Override to create custom behaviour

member.name: T:UIFramework.Adapters.PrefListAdapter
member.summary: Main body of the UI. Lists individual preferences

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.PrefListAdapter.SaveAction
member.summary: When the save button is clicked, the selected category save action will be called. The model is now in charge of what that means

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.TabButtonController.OnSelect
member.summary: Runs when the button is clicked. Implement this in inheriting classes.
member.remarks: IL2CPP does not like abstract methods 😭

member.name: T:UIFramework.Adapters.IChildable
member.summary: Controllers for models that can be submodels of other models. Preference Entries ModButtonView Buttons CategoryTabView Buttons

member.name: P:UIFramework.Adapters.IChildable.Model
member.summary: Reference to the model the controller works from.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.WindowCoordinator.BuildModList
member.summary: Resets the containers and build the modlist

member.name: M:UIFramework.Adapters.WindowCoordinator.SaveButtonClick
member.summary: Gets called when the save button gets clicked.
member.remarks: Generally Melonpreferences are saved by category. But this iterates through the child controllers and call their SaveAction() method. This allows for custom behavior before the actual category gets saved

member.name: T:UIFramework.Models.SelectableModelBase
member.summary: Models that represent buttons on the sidebar and topbar

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.SelectableModelBase.SubModels
member.summary: List of submodels for the model

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.SelectableModelBase.GetSubmodel(System.String)
member.summary: General submodel finder.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.SelectableModelBase.AddSubmodel(UIFramework.Models.IModelable[])
member.summary: Add a new submodel to the list

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.SelectableModelBase.DiscardAction
member.summary: Called when the discard button is pressed

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.ModModelBase.SaveAction
member.summary: Calls individual category models' SaveAction method.

member.name: E:UIFramework.Models.ModModelBase.OnModSaved
member.summary: Subscribe to this event to run code after all the categories for the mod have been saved. This will only run if your mod is the currently selelcted mod.

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.EntryModelBase.OnUICreated
member.summary: Called when the corresponding UI element is created

member.name: T:UIFramework.Models.DataEntryModelBase
member.summary: A model for interfacing with a piece of data.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.DataEntryModelBase.GetNewUIInstance
member.summary: Returns an instance of the game object associated with the MelonPreferences_Entry type. If a custom one is provided, it will return an instance of that instead
member.returns: TODO: Move this to the UI builders as this is ui logic

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.ButtonEntry.BoxedValue
member.summary: This is only to satisfy the contract for IEntry.

member.name: T:UIFramework.Models.IModelable
member.summary: Implemented by all models

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.IModelable.Identifier
member.summary: DisplayName for the model

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.IModelable.DisplayName
member.summary: User-facing display name. Should return Identifier if not assigned to a value

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.IModelable.GetNewUIInstance
member.summary: Instantiates a new Game object associated with them model

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.IModelable.SaveAction
member.summary: Should be called when save button is pressed. Runs after all ancestor's save actions have been run

member.name: T:UIFramework.Models.IHoldSubmodels
member.summary: Models that contain submodels. Generally these are mods and categories representing tabs

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.IHoldSubmodels.SubModels
member.summary: A list of submodels

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.IHoldSubmodels.GetSubmodel(System.String)
member.summary: Finds submodel by identifier

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.IHoldSubmodels.AddSubmodel(UIFramework.Models.IModelable[])
member.summary: Adds a new submodel to the SubModels list

member.name: T:UIFramework.Models.IEntry
member.summary: Goes on the main panel. Contains controls for manipulating preferences or just general UI controls

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.IEntry.Identifier
member.summary: DisplayName/ID of the entry

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.IEntry.Description
member.summary: DisplayName of the entry

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.IEntry.SaveAction
member.summary: Ideally called by the controller to define a save action

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.IEntry.OnUICreated
member.summary: Called when the PrefEntryAdapter has been created. Useful when you wanna change specific elements after creation

member.name: T:UIFramework.Models.IStorable
member.summary: implemented by models that store data

member.name: T:UIFramework.Models.RootModel
member.summary:
Models define how the UI is built. The heirarchy is simple and follows melonpreferences basic structure MelonModel -> MelonCategoryModel -> MelonEntryModel Modders can use the default model just by calling UIF.Register(modInstance, categories) in their OnLateInitializeMelon. The default model will use simple input methods: bools will be toggles, strings will be text input fields and so would numerics. More options will eventually be available: sliders, dropdowns, multi checkboxes, radio buttons, etc.
Those will be developed after the default model is functional

member.name: F:UIFramework.Models.MelonCategoryModel.PrefCat
member.summary: The MelonPreferences_Category object this adapts into the framework

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.MelonCategoryModel.#ctor(MelonLoader.MelonPreferences_Category,UIFramework.Models.ModModelBase)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of this class based on a MelonPreferences_Category

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.MelonEntryModel.PrefEntry
member.summary: MelonPreferences_Entry this model is meant to adapt

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.MelonEntryModel.ModelBoxedValue
member.summary: Direct access to the PrefEntry boxedvalue property

member.name: M:UIFramework.Models.MelonEntryModel.#ctor(MelonLoader.MelonPreferences_Entry,UIFramework.Models.CategoryModelBase)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of this object based around a MelonPreferences_Entry object

member.name: P:UIFramework.Models.MelonEntryModel.SavedValue
member.summary: The value actually saved to the file.

member.name: T:UIFramework.UIFModel
member.summary: These are shim classes to keep backwards compatibility with old models. They don't do anything and just inherit from the new models so that existing mods don't break using the old .Register function

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.DefaultValidator
member.summary: Default implementation of the MelonLoader ValueValidator class. This satisfies the required members but just acts as a passthrough. It's the equivalent of not having a validator at all, but it allows for the use of the other descriptor interfaces without needing to implement a custom validator.

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.SliderDescriptor
member.summary: Default implementation of ISliderDescriptor. Used for numeric inputs that want to be sliders. DecimalPlaces defaults to 5, Min defaults to 0, Max defaults to 1.

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.SliderDescriptor.Min
member.summary: Minimum value. Defaults 0

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.SliderDescriptor.Max
member.summary: Max value. Defaults 1.

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.SliderDescriptor.DecimalPlaces
member.summary: Decimal Places. Defaults 5

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.UserEditNotifier
member.summary: Default implementation of IUserEditedNotifier Use this if you wanna be informed of edits made by the user that aren't applied to the Value property yet

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.DefaultRefreshInhibitor
member.summary:
Default implementation of IRefreshInhibitor Prevent entry from triggering a refresh when a user edits values in the UI or when the entry value changes in code Useful for making sure the UI doesn't refresh while the user is actively editing an entry
This might be removed in a future version. Use this only if you can't find a way to prevent the UI from updating when the user uses a control that has continuous triggers (e.g. sliders) and you can't find a way to defer value application (e.g. using an event trigger for OnPointerUp)
member.remarks: These prevent the entry from causing the UI to refresh This does not mean the entry prevents interruptions from refreshes

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.ITextInputBehaviorDescriptor
member.summary: Describes properties for text inputs that define its behavior.

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.ITextInputBehaviorDescriptor.ContentType
member.summary: What type of content is going into the textinput

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.ITextInputAppearanceDescriptor
member.summary: Describes properties of text inputs that define how it looks

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.ITextInputAppearanceDescriptor.IsAutoSizing
member.summary: Set to true to have the text auto size between AutoSizeMin and AutoSizeMax. If false, FontSize will be used as the font size.

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.IDynamicDropdownDescriptor
member.summary: Presents the entry as a dropdown and describes the options within it.

member.name: M:UIFramework.UiExtensions.IDynamicDropdownDescriptor.SetDropdownItems(System.Collections.Generic.List{UIFramework.UiExtensions.DropdownItem})
member.summary:
Sets the items to be displayed in the dropdown.
When doing a custom implementation, make sure to fire OnDropdownItemsUpdated after storing your data

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.INumericUpDownDescriptor
member.summary: Describes numeric up down controls

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.INumericUpDownDescriptor.Increments
member.summary: 0 = default (1 for ints, 0.1 for floats).

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.ISliderDescriptor
member.summary: Implementing this will present the entry as a slider in the UI

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.IUserEditedNotifier
member.summary: Use this if you wanna be informed of edits made by the user that aren't applied to the Value property yet

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.IUserEditedNotifier.OnUserEdit
member.summary: Subscribe to this action the method you want to run when the edits a value in the UI. It must take an object parameter for the new value

member.name: T:UIFramework.UiExtensions.IRefreshInhibitor
member.summary:
Prevent entry from triggering a refresh when a user edits values in the UI or when the entry value changes in code Useful for making sure the UI doesn't refresh while the user is actively editing an entry
This might be removed in a future version. Use this only if you can't find a way to prevent the UI from updating when the user uses a control that has continuous triggers (e.g. sliders) and you can't find a way to defer value application (e.g. using an event trigger for OnPointerUp)
member.remarks: These prevent the entry from causing the UI to refresh This does not mean the entry prevents interruptions from refreshes

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.IRefreshInhibitor.InhibitRefreshOnEdit
member.summary: Prevents the UI from automatically refreshing when the user edits an entry Use this when the entry involves a control that has continuous input with no easy way to detect when user input has ended

member.name: P:UIFramework.UiExtensions.IRefreshInhibitor.InhibitRefreshOnValueChange
member.summary:
Prevents the UI from refreshing when the value of the entry changes in the background. Use this on entries where your code might change its values while the user is using the UI
This also means that the entry's value won't be reflected in the UI.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Core.UiToggleInputCheck
member.summary: Run OnUpdate. Check if the inputs for toggling the UI have been pressed.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Core.InputToggled(System.Boolean)
member.summary: The actual coroutine that toggles the UI. Has to be in a coroutine to allow for a delay if ModUI is present. If ModUI is present, it matches UI Framework with ModUI. A delay is needed to make sure ModUI's new state has been apllied first before it's copied
member.remarks: This is for ModUI Compatibility. If ModUI is installed, it ensures that UI Framework and ModUI always stay in sync

member.name: M:UIFramework.Core.SyncModUIState
member.summary: Runs after mod UI keyboard toggle to make sure lastModUIState variable is correct

member.name: M:UIFramework.Core.VRActivationAction
member.summary: Checks if activation by VR input has been pressed

member.name: M:UIFramework.Core.FirstGymLoad
member.summary: Called on first gymload to build the UI and find the modUI window.

member.name: M:UIFramework.Debug.DiffLog(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Call in OnUpdate to monitor variables per frame but only logs if they change

member.name: T:UIFramework.UI
member.summary: primary public facing class, modders will interact with this to register their preferences and build the UI.

member.name: M:UIFramework.UI.Register(MelonLoader.MelonMod,MelonLoader.MelonPreferences_Category[])
member.summary: Registers a mod and its categories to the UI instance.
member.param: The range of MelonPreferences_Category objects used by your mod.
member.remarks:
Unless the dependency was explicitly declared, Don't use before OnLateInitializeMelon. Currently, registration after the first gym load means your mod won't show up on the mod list
Deprecated: this version of the Register function will be removed in a future version in favor of taking in the MelonBase type instead Please explicitly cast your mod instance as MelonBase to prevent future incompatibility So UI.Register((MelonBase)this, Category1, Category2,...);
member.returns: A reference to the created ModButtonView Model for further customization

member.name: M:UIFramework.UI.Register(MelonLoader.MelonBase,MelonLoader.MelonPreferences_Category[])
member.summary: Registers a mod or a plugin to UIFramework along with its categories.
member.remarks: Unless the dependency was explicitly declared, Don't use before OnLateInitializeMelon. Currently, registration after the first gym load means your mod won't show up on the mod list

member.name: M:UIFramework.UI.Register(MelonLoader.MelonMod)
member.summary: Registers a mod with no categories to the framework. Categories need to be manually added

member.name: M:UIFramework.UI.Register(MelonLoader.MelonBase)
member.summary: Registers a mod or plugin with no categories to the framework. Categories need to be manually added

member.name: F:UIFramework.Prefabs.UIFGameObjects
member.summary: Root Game Object for all of UI in DDOL

member.name: F:UIFramework.Prefabs.AssetBundleLoaded
member.summary: UI Assets from the asset bundle

member.name: F:UIFramework.Prefabs.TempStorage
member.summary: Temporary game object storage as they're being instantiated

member.name: T:UIFramework.Helpers
member.summary: contains various helper functions ToNormal: Normalizes a string GetDisplayNames

member.name: M:UIFramework.Helpers.ToNormal(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a normalized version of the specified string by converting it to lowercase, trimming whitespace, and removing all spaces.
member.param: The string to normalize. Cannot be null.
member.returns: A normalized string with all spaces removed, trimmed, and converted to lowercase.
member.remarks: Returns empty string if input only contains whitespace

member.name: T:UIFramework.Preferences
member.summary: UI's own preferences go here
